hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The solver must discover that evolution fails to prune stale edges because the graph walker skips unvisited nodes.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: The solver must synthesize graph walker edge deletion, action signature tracking in fingerprints, and manifest-db synchronization.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Requires diagnosing structural convergence faults across multi-file outputs rather than standard syntax or configuration bugs.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: The action graph, build database, manifest, and outputs are tightly coupled, meaning changes to one affect convergence.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The convergence failure is an abstract graph mutation challenge, which avoids typical training dataset patterns.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The instruction specifies only external requirements without disclosing internal scheduling, hashing, or walker algorithms.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: All correctness contracts are documented, preventing hidden check failures.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires coordinate edits across the graph module, fingerprinting module, and manifest module.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests verify multi-step sequences of mutations that require generalized graph traversal fixes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Symptoms match the actual failure behavior of the scheduling and graph evolution components.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is based on structural dependency evolution rather than timing checks, obfuscated tokens, or sandbox limits.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Simple search-and-replace or single line tweaks are insufficient to fix the coupled graph reconciliation errors.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The fix frontier spans multiple files across the graph, scheduler, and manifest sub-packages.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The fix frontier files align exactly with the core components being debugged.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires structural Go coding changes across the three modules rather than config updates.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Planned symbols and path structures are named normally and do not reveal the exact patch site.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Decoys and helper methods require full logical reasoning rather than obvious replacement blocks.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The instructions do not outline step-by-step commands to repair the scheduler.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security, auth, or sandbox isolation mechanics are used to inflate difficulty.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: All verifier checkpoints are directly related to incremental dependency resolution and convergence.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The test suite invokes the build command directly without custom test harness wrappers.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Solver cannot satisfy the verifier by mocking outputs because subsequent edit phases invalidate previous intermediate mock states.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires solving complex state updates and dependency evolution graphs, placing it in the hard difficulty tier.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Discovery of the exact walker graph logic and interface export fingerprinting is left to the solver.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No algorithms, keys, or CLI details are overdisclosed.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The topology correctly lists all structural files that are checked by the verifier.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Each criterion is mapped to deterministic test execution and manifest comparisons.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: All observable properties and files are clearly defined in the instruction.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Correct Go code in the three modules satisfies all verifier runs.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Requires structural fixes across multiple components, which is difficult for SOTA models.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Models real-world compiler and build tool scheduling divergence problems.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The verifier checks database status, graph edges, and manifest content convergence.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: The instructions tell the solver what the required behavior is, but the solver must debug the Go codebase to understand why dependency edges, manifests, and fingerprints diverge.

discovery_budget.discovery: Dependency graph evolution stale edge removal logic bug location
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing the exact file and line for edge deletion turns a graph-logic repair into a copy-paste patch.

discovery_budget.discovery: Fingerprint hashing exception for interface exports
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing the exact fingerprinting struct or method would expose the scheduler repair path.
Revealing how the manifest gets out of sync with the database would bypass the stateful reconstruction challenge.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Instructions describe only the external symptoms (developer reports, build commands, file structure) and the expected convergence correctness properties.

attack_path.summary: Fix stale edge removal in dag.go, fix action fingerprint update in fingerprint.go, and update registry reconciliation in registry.go.
attack_path.reasoning: The solver must identify that stale edges are retained after edits because the graph walker skips unvisited edges under sequential deletes, correct the action fingerprint logic to evaluate exported Go interface metadata, and synchronize database entries during manifest updates.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Approximately 45-60 lines of Go code changes across the three modules.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Requires adding edge deletion logic on unvisited edges (15 lines), incorporating interface signature hash in scheduler fingerprints (20 lines), and writing registry synchronization queries (15 lines).

collapse_audit.reasoning: The three bugs are logically independent and verified across separate steps of the sequential verifier test loop. Solving only one does not result in a passing verifier.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Requires understanding how graph mutations propagate through scheduling, serialization, and database states.

topology_enumeration.summary: The serialized DAG of actions and dependency edges.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: The action graph structure is validated by the verifier, built by the graph module, and serialized to disk.

topology_enumeration.summary: The database caching action fingerprints and build state.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: The DB holds fingerprints which must converge with actual action executions.

topology_enumeration.summary: The JSON file recording generated build targets and content digests.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: The manifest must converge with the graph, which is verified by cross-checking.

symbol_table.purpose: Updates dependency graph edges, pruning stale links correctly.
Computes fingerprint of a build action, including Go interface signatures.

symbol_table.signature: func SyncRegistry(dbPath string, manifestPath string) error
symbol_table.purpose: Synchronizes current physical build outputs and registry manifest with database state.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Coordinates build execution threads (decoy scheduler subsystem).
Performs topological sorting of graph nodes for execution queues.
Initializes SQLite connection pool and defines tables.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Single component patches may compile and pass simple initial build tests but fail on subsequent downstream package edits.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Model assumes a local test pass guarantees complete graph convergence.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Test suite runs sequential build cycles checking graph serialization.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Verifier performs mutations, cleanups, restarts, and checks outputs sequentially.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Model only fixes the first-step compilation or manifest edit.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Step descriptions are mapped to build guarantees.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Reconciles action_graph.json, artifact_manifest.json, build.db, and generated outputs.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Model edits only one output instead of resolving scheduler/manifest code.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Convergence contract is detailed in the build contract document.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Cached artifacts appear correct on clean rebuild but diverge after incremental runs.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Model rebuilds cleanly or clears caches to bypass debugging the graph walker.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Instructions state that complete rebuilds succeed but deterministic incremental behavior must be preserved.

calibration_plan.human_sanity: Verified that sequential Go rebuilds and json diffs trigger on bugs.
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: Clearing the cache directory fails incremental consistency tests.
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Ablating fingerprint checks and verifying failures on downstream triggers.

pass_rate_target.basis: Worst-model pass rate on platform auto-eval.

metrics.criterion: Passes all incremental build convergence phases.
Dependency graph correctly prunes stale edges.
Downstream targets correctly trigger on interface change.
Manifest matches build outputs and database state.

task_shape.hardness_source: Sequential graph edits, fingerprint evolution mismatch, and multi-file convergence.
task_shape.collapse_risk: Naïve fixes to single components fail multi-file convergence check.

category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: build command, output locations, externally observable build guarantees, output schemas, deterministic behavior requirements, regeneration requirements
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: patch location, scheduler name, fingerprint algorithm, dependency traversal implementation, graph serialization details
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Verifier performs multiple sequential mutations, restarts, and check steps.

satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: Medium (avoid graph terminology matching implementation identifiers)

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: This task exercises incremental state consistency and scheduling in build engines.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Bazel/Buck style interface fingerprinting and graph caching issues.
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: Represents standard incremental DAG evolution challenges.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: Graph walker, fingerprinting functions, manifest output loop.
